response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with genuine IDE-internals exposure (built C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI, a commercial C IDE), which aligns with the language-tooling spirit of the role. However, the core technical requirements are a poor match: the role demands strong Java and Kotlin with deep JVM understanding plus solid Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, while this candidate's depth is in DLang, Node.js/TypeScript, and web/mobile work. Java appears only in passing (robotic arms, device orchestration) and is a skills-list item rather than a demonstrated specialty; Kotlin, Spring, and JVM internals are absent entirely.
strengths: Direct IDE-tooling experience building C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE
Senior breadth across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded with strong testing/TDD discipline
Proven work on high-throughput, large-scale systems (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, both core requirements
Java exposure is shallow and dated; no evidence of deep JVM understanding
Primary expertise (DLang, Node/TypeScript) sits outside the JVM enterprise focus
